# UC-14.3.61 · Aranet Atmospheric Pressure Delta — Door or Filter Breach Indicator

## Description

Sudden pressure steps in zoned facilities can indicate opened airlocks, blown filters, or failed dampers when correlated with access control. Aranet atmospheric pressure sensors sampled every few minutes make a coarse differential-pressure proxy when dedicated DP transmitters are not wired to BMS.

## Value

Adds environmental corroboration for physical security and contamination controls without installing separate DP wiring everywhere.

## Implementation

Requires stable indoor placement away from weather-dominated entries; pair with outdoor reference sensor subtracted in `eval` if available. `streamstats` window=6 assumes ~30 minutes of 5-minute polls—adjust for your cadence. Tune `delta_hpa` (hPa) to facility noise floor; correlate with door badge and HVAC mode changes to reduce false positives.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Reference sensor or weather feed optional but strongly recommended.
• Document HVAC economizer cycles that move pressure.

Step 1 — Backtest window on a year of data.

Step 2 — Add `by zone_id` if multiple sensors per room.

Step 3 — Physical security runbook cross-links.

Step 4 — Privacy review if combined with badge data.

## SPL

```spl
index=environment sourcetype=aranet:sensor sensor_id=* pressure_hpa=*
| sort 0 _time
| streamstats window=6 global=f earliest(pressure_hpa) as p0 latest(pressure_hpa) as p1 by sensor_id
| eval delta_hpa=round(p1-p0,2)
| where abs(delta_hpa) > 2.5
| table _time, sensor_id, location, p0, p1, delta_hpa
| eval abs_delta=abs(delta_hpa)
| sort - abs_delta
```

## Visualization

Line chart (pressure_hpa), Timeline (alerts), Overlay with door access logs via `join`.

## References

- [Aranet — air quality and atmospheric pressure sensors](https://aranet.com/)
